message.content:
Here’s a draft you can adapt:

---

We’re not building a revolution. We’re building a ruler.

Freelancers already do the hard, creative, chaotic work; we’re not here to “change the world” for them. We’re here to quietly measure it so they can shape it on purpose.

Right now, money for freelancers feels like weather: it comes in gusts, dries up without warning, and is mostly something you react to. Our vision is to turn that weather into a calendar—something you can see ahead of time, plan around, and use to decide what to say yes or no to.

Our app is a simple, honest tool: it tracks what comes in, what goes out, and what’s likely to happen next. But the meaning behind it is this:

- So that a designer can decide to take a week off without guessing.  
- So that a developer can say no to a bad client and know it won’t sink them.  
- So that a writer can choose a personal project and still pay rent.

We’re not promising to fix the economy or “empower every creator on Earth.” Our vision is smaller and more practical:  
to become the quiet instrument freelancers use to turn uncertain income into intentional decisions.

If we do that well—and only if we do that well—our product will matter. Not because it changes everything, but because it helps real people change one important thing: how confidently they live with the work they’ve already chosen.
